{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",

    "installed-defective"

  "percent_complete": 95,

  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Missing end-of-line (EOL) resistor on the fire alarm initiating device circuit (IDC) or notification appliance circuit (NAC)."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install the correct value end-of-line resistor at the last device on the circuit as specified by the manufacturer and system drawings.",
    "Retest the circuit to verify proper supervisory monitoring and fault detection."
  ],
  "rationale": "A missing end-of-line resistor prevents the fire alarm control panel from supervising the integrity of the circuit wiring, meaning a break in the wire would not be detected as a trouble condition, violating NFPA 72 requirements.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 72 Chapter 12",
    "NFPA 72 Section 12.3"
  ]
}
